name: Emilian–Romagnol
notes: Emilian-Romagnol (emiliàn e rumagnòl), a Gallo-Italic group of northern Italy. COMMITMENT: this map implements ONE variety and ONE norm — BOLOGNESE (Emilian side) written in the Ortografia Lessicografica Moderna (OLM) of Vitali & Lepri. Emilian-Romagnol is a dialect continuum with several competing city norms; Romagnol proper (and other Emilian cities) use different letter values and are NOT covered — a Romagnol-specific spec is the honest unit of future work. INPUT CONTRACT: lowercase Bolognese text in OLM spelling, with its diacritics intact (â å ä è ê î ò ô û, and the dotted sibilants ṡ / ż). Vowel length and quality are carried entirely by the diacritics, so stripping them destroys the mapping. Key OLM values: <z> = /θ/ and <ż> = /ð/ (NOT Italian /ts dz/); <s> = /s/ vs <ṡ> = /z/; <n> before a velar = [ŋ]; <c> and <g> are /k ɡ/ hard and /ts dz/ soft (before front vowels); <gn> = /ɲ/, <gli> = /ʎ/. Note the English Wikipedia 'Emilian language' article gives an incompatible Bolognese consonant chart (/tʃ dʒ/ and front rounded vowels); the OLM-anchored 'Bolognese dialect' table is followed here.

ancestors.notes: Romance — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's computed centroid for the Emiliano-Romagnolo family — a summary of a whole clade's range, not a place anyone speaks.
